MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
W. Joseph Gunter, Esq., of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Oakfabco, Inc.
James P. Nevin, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Charles Sumner, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT OAKFABCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Oakfabco, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Ms. Pulis was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Purported undisputed material fact 12 in defendant's separate statement states that the deposition testimony of James Dorris is the 'only case-specific' witness or document identified by plaintiffs in their contention discovery responses that Ms. Pulis was exposed to asbestos from a product attributable to defendant. Among the evidence cited to support this purported undisputed fact is exhibits B and C to Mr. Stewart's declaration submitted with the moving papers. Page 4 lines 1 and 2 of Exhibit C show that the purported undisputed fact 12 is not correct. In those lines plaintiffs identify two persons (Jack Davis and Tommy Peck) who are nowhere discussed in defendant's separate statement. While it may be that defendant has made reasonable efforts to locate and contact Mr. Davis and Mr. Peck and been unsuccessful, there is no evidence to that effect. The court further heard and granted defendant's application to continue the trial date. Trial date of April 4, 2011 continued to October 3, 2011 at 11:15 a.m. in Dept. 220. Counsel for defendant is to prepare two separte orders for Court's signatures. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
